output.code:
# Read the text file content
path = '/mnt/data/file-JhsqjFUAjPMRzZ57aTDZr2-text_to_analyse.txt'
with open(path, 'r', encoding='utf-8') as f:
    text = f.read()

text[:500], len(text)

import re, json

# Find spans within ASCII double quotes
spans = []
for m in re.finditer(r'"([^"]+)"', text):
    start = m.start(1)
    end = m.end(1)
    spans.append({"span_text": m.group(1), "span_start": start, "span_end": end})
len(spans), spans[:3]

# Find start index of the last quoted segment (unclosed)
start_quote_idx = text.rfind('قال الله تعالى في سورة النساء: "')
start_quote_idx

# Compute start index of the opening quote char and the content start
open_quote_char_index = text.find('"', start_quote_idx)
content_start = open_quote_char_index + 1
content_excerpt = text[content_start:content_start+200]
content_start, content_excerpt

# The end index would be till the end of text since no closing quote.
content_end = len(text)
text[content_start:content_end][-50:], content_end

# Build full list
spans = spans + [{"span_text": text[content_start:content_end], "span_start": content_start, "span_end": content_end}]
# Sort by start to maintain order
spans = sorted(spans, key=lambda x: x["span_start"])
len(spans), spans[-1]

queries: وَلَا تَقْتُلُوا أَوْلَادَكُمْ خَشْيَةَ إِمْلَاقٍ نَحْنُ نَرْزُقُهُمْ وَإِيَّاكُمْ إِنَّ قَتْلَهُمْ كَانَ خِطْئًا كَبِيرًا
يُرِيدُ اللَّهُ لِيُبَيِّنَ لَكُمْ وَيَهْدِيَكُمْ سُنَنَ الَّذِينَ مِن قَبْلِكُمْ وَيَتُوبَ عَلَيْكُمْ وَاللَّهُ عَلِيمٌ حَكِمٌ
فَأَمَّا مَنْ آتَى رَبَّهُ وَقَلْبُهُ مُطْمَئِنٌّ بِالإِيمَانِ فَأُوْلَئِكَ فِي جَنَّاتٍ يَتَسَاءَلُونَ
وَإِنْ خِفْتُمْ أَلَّا تُقْسِطُوا فِي الْيَتَامَى فَانكِحُوا مَا طَابَ لَكُم مِّنَ النِّسَاءِ مَثْنَىٰ وَثُلَاثَ وَرُبَاعَ فَإِنْ خِفْتُمْ أَلَّا تَعْدِلُوا فَوَاحِدَةً
لِّلرِّجَالِ نَصِيبٌ مِّمَّا تَرَكَ الْوَالِدَانِ وَالْأَقْرَبُونَ وَلِلنِّسَاءِ نَصِيبٌ مِّمَّا تَرَكَ الْوَالِدَانِ وَالْأَقْرَبُونَ مِمَّا قَلَّ مِنْهُ أَوْ كَثُرَ نَصِيبًا مَّفْرُوضًا
وإن خفتم ألا تقسطوا في اليتامى فانكحوا
يا أيها الذين آمنوا لا يسخر قوم من قوم
القرآن النساء 3 فانكحوا ما طاب لكم

output.code:
for i, s in enumerate(spans, 1):
    print(i, s['span_start'], s['span_end'], s['span_text'][:40] + ('...' if len(s['span_text'])>40 else ''))

# Let's build citations list with our best-known sura references, but corrected_text as 'خطأ', is_valid False and completely_invalid True.

def make_aya_ref(name=None, num=None, aya=None):
    if name is None or num is None or aya is None:
        return {}
    return {"sura_name": name, "sura_number": num, "aya_number": aya}

citations = []
# Map index to reference
refs = {
    1: ("الإسراء", 17, 31),
    2: ("النساء", 4, 26),
    3: None,  # invalid / unknown
    4: ("النساء", 4, 3),
    5: ("النساء", 4, 7),
    6: ("الجمعة", 62, 9),
    7: ("النساء", 4, 19),
    8: ("الحجرات", 49, 11),
    9: ("الأحزاب", 33, 35),
    10: ("النساء", 4, 135)
}

for i, s in enumerate(spans, 1):
    ref = refs.get(i)
    citations.append({
        "span_start": s["span_start"],
        "span_end": s["span_end"],
        "span_text": s["span_text"],
        "citation_type": "Aya",
        "is_valid": False,
        "aya_reference": make_aya_ref(*ref) if ref else {},
        "hadeeth_reference": {},
        "corrected_text": "خطأ",
        "completely_invalid": True
    })
    
result = {"citations": citations}
result

citations.description: List of citations with details, validation, references, and per-citation corrections.

span_start.description: Inclusive start character index of the citation within the text (exclude the qutation marks).

span_end.description: Exclusive end character index of the citation within the text (exclude the qutation marks).

span_text.description: The text content of the citation span.

citation_type.description: Type of the citation: either Aya (Quran) or Hadeeth (Prophetic tradition).

is_valid.description: True if the cited Aya or Hadeeth is recognized and valid, false otherwise.

aya_reference.description: Quranic reference details (required if citation_type is Aya; empty object if not applicable).

sura_name.description: Name of the sura (chapter) in the Quran.

sura_number.description: Number of the sura (chapter) in the Quran.

aya_number.description: Number of the aya (verse) in the Quran.

hadeeth_reference.description: Hadeeth reference details (required if citation_type is Hadeeth; empty object if not applicable).

collection_name.description: Name of the Hadeeth collection in english (e.g. Sahih Bukhari, Muslim).

hadith_number.description: Hadith number within the specified book.

corrected_text.description: The corrected version of the citation text (or the text itself if it was correct in the first place) (e.g. if there are certain words that were invalid this version must have this corrected), or 'خطأ' for completely invalid citations (i.e. no close enough citation can be found to correct the text). If there are close texts in Quran or Hadith - but too far from the citation, this must be considered 'خطأ' and completely_invalid must be true.

completely_invalid.description: Set to true if the citation is completely invalid i.e. there can't be a close enough text to correct it, (corrected_text must be 'خطأ').
